name: Cabbage Crown
recipeInstructions:
1. In a large bowl of an electric mixer, combine ground beef, flour, salt, pepper, and egg. Beat at medium speed until blended. Gradually beat in milk, one tablespoon at a time, until smooth and paste-like. Stir in onion.
2. Trim the outer leaves from the cabbage. Cut a slice about an inch thick from the core end; set aside. Remove the core from the cabbage with a sharp knife, then hollow out the cabbage to make a shell approximately half an inch thick. (Chop the removed pieces coarsely and cook as a vegetable for another day.)
3. Spoon the meat mixture into the shell. Fit the cut slice back in place. Secure tightly with soft kitchen string.
4. Place the stuffed cabbage, core end down, in the slow cooker. Add boiling water to the cooker.
5. Cook on low for 8 hours or on high for 4 hours, until the cabbage is tender. Remove from heat and keep warm.
6. In a cup, combine 3 tablespoons flour and 1/3 cup cold water. Pour into the liquid in the slow cooker; cover. Cook at high heat for 15 minutes. Season to taste with salt and pepper. Darken with a few drops of bottled gravy coloring.
7. Place the stuffed cabbage on a heated serving platter and remove the string. Pour the gravy into a separate bowl. Cut the cabbage into wedges and spoon gravy over each piece.
